heading: Grants for programs for drug-endangered children
label: 10663 Grants for programs for drug-endangered children
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Combat Methamphetamine Epidemic Act of 2005, and also as part of the USA PATRIOT Improvement and Reauthorization Act of 2005, and not as part of title I of the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 which comprises this chapter.Section was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section. Some section numbers or references in amendment notes below reflect the classification of such sections or references prior to editorial reclassification.section 3797cc–2 of Title 42

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Attorney General shall make grants to States, territories, and Indian tribes (as defined in ) for the purpose of carrying out programs to provide comprehensive services to aid children who are living in a home in which methamphetamine or other controlled substances are unlawfully manufactured, distributed, dispensed, or used.section 10554 of this title

Coordination among law enforcement agencies, prosecutors, child protective services, social services, health care services, and any other services determined to be appropriate by the Attorney General to provide assistance regarding the problems of children described in subsection (a).
Transition of children from toxic or drug-endangering environments to appropriate residential environments.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: c Authorization of appropriations
children.text: For the purpose of carrying out this section, there are authorized to be appropriated $20,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 2008 and 2009. Amounts appropriated under the preceding sentence shall remain available until expended.
